L1.area: Distributed validator signing and consensus vote management

L2.description: A race condition exists in the consensus signature state handling where concurrent signing requests for the same block height, round, and step can both pass a safety check before either updates the shared state. The check and update operations use separate locking scopes, creating a TOCTOU (time-of-check-to-time-of-use) gap that allows conflicting signatures to be produced and broadcast for the same block, constituting a double-sign protocol violation that results in stake slashing penalties.

L3.area: Cosigner signature state tracking and high watermark persistence
L3.description: A race condition exists in the cosigner's signature state handling where the safety check against the current high watermark (height-round-step) and the subsequent state update use separate locking scopes. When two concurrent sign requests arrive for the same height-round-step, both can pass the safety check under their respective locks before either writes the updated state, creating a TOCTOU gap. This allows conflicting signatures (e.g., a non-nil and a nil block ID vote) to be produced for the same block, constituting a double-sign violation in the Cosmos validator protocol.

ground_truth.reason: The Save method performed a safety check and state update under separate locks, allowing two concurrent signing requests for the same height-round-step to both pass the check before either wrote the updated state, resulting in a double-sign.
